title: Blank nodes not allowed with SPARQL 1.1. `INSERT DATA`?
description:
INSERT DATA {_:node18errd605x19406 <http://www.w3.org/2006/vcard/ns#postalCode> """67401""" .}
results in
SP031: SPARQL compiler: Blank node '_:node18errd605x19406' is not allowed in a constant clause
INSERT {_:node18errd605x19406 <http://www.w3.org/2006/vcard/ns#postalCode> """67401""" .} is OK.
However, in the SPARQL 1.1 INSERT DATA specification I don't see any reason why this should be disallowed. The same states this answer
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), postal_code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, name, postal_code) VALUES (1, 'John Doe', '67401');
INSERT INTO test_data (id, name, postal_code) VALUES (2, 'Jane Smith', NULL);
UPDATE test_data SET postal_code = '99999' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE postal_code IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Blank nodes not allowed in SPARQL INSERT DATA
